summary: A solid, clean Dead show with standout Cold Rain & Snow and a superb Scarlet instrumental; recording quality not described but appears serviceable.
review: The concert is remembered as a good‑clean, standard Grateful Dead performance – highlights include an inspired Cold Rain and Snow opener, a blistering Scarlet instrumental, and a nicely executed Loose Lucy.  The Fire reprise fell short for some listeners, and the second set lacked the momentum of earlier ’91 shows, but overall fans describe the night positively.  No specific recording‑quality details are given, suggesting the tape is at least listenable.

ai_rating.rationale: Three reviews (two 4‑star, one 3‑star) praise the musical performance with a few criticisms; no recording issues are mentioned, so the overall rating reflects a good show on an acceptable recording.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers do not comment on soundboard vs. audience sources or clarity; they focus on performance, implying the audio is adequate but unremarkable.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set is described as a clean, standard Dead show with strong first‑set material; the second half lacked the usual fire and momentum of the famed ’91 run, resulting in an overall steady but not spectacular pacing.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Fans recall his friendly stage presence and interaction, though no specific musical comments are made.
band_member_comments.Phil: Plays a Fire riff during the Terrapin jam that goes unheard by the band.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince (keyboards) is praised for coming into his own during this run of shows.
